template.p: Welcome to the Odoo Event Management package!

This suite of applications is designed specifically for businesses in the events industry; whether you're organizing corporate retreats, outdoor team-buildings, workshops & seminars or sports challenges. Odoo brings together everything you need to manage your business in one place: from receiving client requests and sending attractive quotations, to scheduling staff, handling logistics, and invoicing clients.

By using this solution, you’ll gain access to essential tools like CRM, Sales, Projects, Rentals, Planning, and more. These apps work seamlessly together to streamline your operations, save time, and improve coordination across your entire team.
Focus on delivering unforgettable events while Odoo takes care of the behind-the-scenes tasks.🎤
You can access every installed App in your Odoo database on your main dashboard.

li.p: Capture leads directly from your website and track them in CRM, where you can follow up, plan meetings, and qualify opportunities.
Send attractive proposals and service offers using Sales app.
Organize your network of venues, suppliers, and partners with detailed profiles in Contacts.
Turn confirmed bookings into structured event plans with theProject app.
Set up and manage event registrations, tickets, and attendee lists using the Events app
Manage logistics and equipment rentals smoothly with Rental and Inventory, ensuring item availability.
Ensure all event equipment stays in top condition by scheduling repairs and maintenance using the Maintenance app.
Use Planning to assign staff for each event and publish new job offers through Recruitment.
Keep your team aligned with shared calendars using Calendar.
Create and send invoices quickly from Invoicing.
Collect digital signatures on quotes or agreements with Sign for faster client approval.

div.class: o_editor_banner user-select-none lh-1 d-flex align-items-center alert alert-info pb-0 pt-3 o-contenteditable-false
w-100 px-3 o_editor_banner_content o-contenteditable-true
div.p: Odoo is entirely integrated into an App. Download it to turn your phone to manage your entire business in it.

template.p: By uploading this module's demo data, your database has been filled with a few contacts.
You should be able to execute the following flows to have an overview of various flows you can quickly execute with this package using Odoo.
Of course, this is just an overview of the features included in this package. Feel free to add new apps, delete/modify demo data, and test everything around!

div.class: o_editor_banner lh-1 d-flex align-items-center alert alert-warning pb-0 pt-3 o-contenteditable-false
w-100 ms-3 o_editor_banner_content o-contenteditable-true
div.p: You didn't import demo data? You can still do it. Go to Apps > Industries > Upgrade your Event Management package and check the related box.

template.p: Create a centralized, searchable database of your vendors, venues, and event partners.

li.p: Open the Contact app:
Add all business contacts: food trucks, venues, entertainers, caterers.
Enter generic info: emails, phone number, specific point of contact for each business, address...
Take detailed notes on each contact, including their price ranges and any other important info to remember.
Log internal reviews or feedback for future events to help evaluate vendor or venue performance, in the chatter of the corresponding contact via the "Log notes" button.
Use tags to organize your contact database efficiently (e.g., Catering, Venue, Photographer).
Add specific tags like “Catering Free: YES” if the venue allows you to choose your own catering service, or “NO” if you must use the venue’s catering.

div.class: o_editor_banner user-select-none o-contenteditable-false lh-1 d-flex align-items-center alert alert-info pb-0 pt-3
o_editor_banner_content o-contenteditable-true w-100 px-3
div.p: Link Knowledge articles or notes with practical info (e.g., parking capacity).

li.p: Save a list of practical info, such as parking details, seating capacity, or available outdoor space, in the Knowledge app’s clipboard.
Attach this information directly to the relevant contact’s notes and complete all essential information to have a comprehensive overview of each venue when planning events.

template.p: Convert incoming requests into event opportunities.

li.p: Create a form on your Website allowing clients to request a quotation for their event (e.g., activity type, participants, ideal period and duration, budget,...).
When submitted, the form generates a new opportunity in the CRM module.
In CRM:
Plan activities like a discovery call, task to check venue availability, or meeting.
Assign a priority level to your opportunity and responsible employee.
Use the chatter to communicate and keep track of updates.

template.p: When an opportunity is complex and involves significant data collection before becoming a Sales Order, use the Gear (⚙️) icon to generate a project directly from the opportunity. This creates a linked Pre-Sales project where you can centralize all useful information, such as vendor prices, official documents, and preliminary schedules. When the order is finalized, your groundwork is already in place, allowing you to move straight into execution.
See it for yourself! (Requires demo data)

li.p: Easy Quotation: In the CRM app, open the opportunity "Rent Tent" from Jessica Martinez

ol.li: Because this is a straightforward request, we created the Rental Order directly from the opportunity. You will see that the rental is currently scheduled as a draft while awaiting confirmation.

Once confirmed, you can mark the opportunity as "Won" and generate the invoices. Odoo will then automatically handle the logistics by creating two transfers—one for delivery and one for the return—ensuring seamless tracking of the tent's availability.

li.p: Complex Project Management: Open the "City Festival – Summer in the Square – Brussels" opportunity.
Due to the complexity of this event, it is already linked to a Project. Within this project, you can view the Purchase Order where we are sourcing catering quotes.
You can build the Sales Order in draft mode, adding items as the requirements evolve.
Once approved, simply use the existing project to manage the execution phase.

template.p: Generate clear, detailed quotations that accurately reflect your services, supplier availability, and timing. As mentioned, these can be initiated directly from a CRM Opportunity or from within a Pre-Sales Project.
To leverage Odoo’s full potential, it is essential to understand how different Product Types impact your workflow. In this demo, we have categorized products as follows:

li.p: Service Products: Used for high-level quoting or purchasing.

blockquote.p: Example: You quote a "100-Person Outdoor Tent." This service isn't tied to a specific stock item yet; depending on availability, you might fulfill this using one large tent or two smaller ones.

li.p: Consumable Products: Items that appear on your picking lists but do not require strict inventory tracking.

blockquote.p: Example: Staff T-shirts or extension cords. You need them for the job, but you don't need to track every individual unit’s movement in and out of the warehouse.

li.p: Storable (Traced) Products: Strategic assets that require exact stock counts at all times.

blockquote.p: Example: A "100m² Tent." Because these are high-demand and limited, Odoo tracks their real-time availability (see the Rental section for more details).

template.p: To create a truly tailor-made proposal, you can utilize:

li.p: Predefined Templates: Speed up your process with standard layouts.
Sections & Notes: Organize your offer and add context for the client.
Terms & Conditions: Ensure legal clarity on every deal.
Discounts: Apply price adjustments directly to line items.

template.p: and many more options! Feel free to check the official documentation for a deeper dive into these features.Sales & Quotations

div.class: o_editor_banner user-select-none o-contenteditable-false lh-1 d-flex align-items-center alert alert-info pb-0 pt-3
o_editor_banner_content o-contenteditable-true w-100 px-3
div.p: We placed headers and footers in the demo data. But do you want to create an attractive quotation by yourself? Watch the “Outstanding Quotations” video to help you build a nice quotation and make your offer even more attractive.

template.p: The Project app serves as the central hub for managing all actions and information tied to an event. Use the smart buttons in the top bar to navigate between linked documents, tasks, and financial data instantly.
Save time by creating Project Templates. Define a standard set of tasks once, then reuse them every time you launch a new project to ensure no detail is overlooked.

li.p: Granular Control: Manage deadlines, assignees, internal notes, and sub-tasks for every stage of the project.
Custom Stages: Tailor project and task stages to align perfectly with your team’s specific internal processes.

template.p: Keep all essential files in the Documents section. This is the single source of truth for:

li.p: Official Paperwork: Contracts and legal authorizations (City, Police, etc.).
Staff Resources: Site plans, emergency contact lists, and event agendas.
Easy Sharing: Simply copy the Share Link for any document and send it to your team along with their schedule (see planning section)

div.class: o_editor_banner user-select-none o-contenteditable-false lh-1 d-flex align-items-center alert alert-info pb-0 pt-3
o_editor_banner_content o-contenteditable-true w-100 px-3
div.p: If you want to track project profitability, use the same filter button as above to display a dashboard on your Project page. This gives you a clear view of costs and revenues to see how profitable each event is.

template.p: Efficiently manage supplier relationships and equipment sourcing. Whether you are ordering for a specific event or restocking your warehouse, Odoo keeps your costs and logistics in one view.

li.p: Integrated Budgeting: Launch a Request for Quotation (RFQ) directly from the project’s smart buttons to ensure all costs are automatically factored into your Project Profitability.
Smart Comparisons: Add alternative products or vendors to a single RFQ. Once you select the best offer, Odoo automatically cancels the other options for you.

template.p: Before confirming your Purchase Order, decide where your goods need to go:

li.p: Internal Warehouse: By default, Odoo assumes items (like new stock) are coming to your warehouse.
Direct-to-Event (Dropshipping): For items that should go straight to the venue, change the "Deliver To" field to "Dropship" and select the event location as the address.

template.p: Use the Stock Moves button within your project to see a unified list of all transfers. You can instantly distinguish between items arriving at your warehouse and those being dropshipped directly to the festival or client site.

div.class: o_editor_banner user-select-none o-contenteditable-false lh-1 d-flex align-items-center alert alert-info pb-0 pt-3
o_editor_banner_content o-contenteditable-true w-100 px-3
div.p: You can add a warnings for your vendors to remind your team of important constraints. For example, “Vendor requires 2 weeks’ notice for large orders” helps ensure timely planning and smooth deliveries.

template.p: To ensure a successful event, you need a crystal-clear view of your equipment. The Rental App is your primary tool for tracking high-value assets and avoiding double-bookings.
You can create Rental Orders for various scenarios:

li.p: Client Rentals: Standard orders with specific pricing and automated invoicing. (like Jessica Martinez in the demo data)
Large-Scale Events: Rentals bundled into a larger project at $0 (since the cost is already covered in the overall package).
Internal Maintenance: Blocks off equipment for repairs or quality checks so it isn't accidentally booked.
The Schedule View: Click on "Schedule" for a timeline of your equipment.

blockquote.p: Tip: Remove the "Confirmed Orders" filter to see pending quotations. This allows you to spot potential conflicts before they happen.

li.p: Conflict Alerts: When creating a rental quotation, a red icon will appear next to a line item if Odoo detects a stock shortage for those dates.

template.p: For every confirmed Rental Order involving traced products, Odoo automatically generates two movements:

li.p: Delivery: From your Warehouse to the Client.
Return: From the Client back to your Warehouse.

template.p: The Stock Moves tab in your project is your command center. It consolidates every movement related to the event:

li.p: Purchases & Dropshipping: Transfers coming from suppliers.
Rentals: Equipment moving to and from the client.

Internal Picking Lists: For items not covered by rentals (like consumables), you can manually create a Picking List. Simply set the Destination to the event location, select your products, and set the date.

template.p: Now, every single item—from a massive stage tent to a box of cables—is tracked and centralized in one view.
Seamlessly transition from hiring to shift management by connecting your Recruitment, Employee, and Planning apps.
Build your event team by managing interim positions (e.g., Hosts, Security, or Activity Instructors) through the Recruitment app:

li.p: Post & Publish: Create job openings and publish them directly to your Odoo website.
Pipeline Management: Track applications, conduct interviews, and hire your team in one centralized dashboard.

template.p: Once a candidate is hired, ensure their Employee Profile is set up for scheduling:

li.p: In the Work Information tab, navigate to the Planning section.
Add their specific Job Position under "Roles." This ensures Odoo knows exactly which shifts they are qualified to fill.

template.p: Efficiently organize your event schedule using these three steps:

li.p: Step 1: Define the Shifts Create all necessary time slots (e.g., 6 staff for "Setup" from 07:00 to 15:00, 4 for "Security" from 10:00 to 16:00).

div.class: o_editor_banner user-select-none o-contenteditable-false lh-1 d-flex align-items-center alert alert-success pb-0 pt-3
o_editor_banner_content o-contenteditable-true w-100 px-3
div.p: Use Shift Templates for recurring needs across different projects to save time.

li.p: Step 2: Assign the Team You can manually drag and drop employees into slots or use the "Auto-Assign" button. Odoo will automatically match staff based on their defined Roles and Availability.
Step 3: Publish & Sync Once the schedule is finalized, click "Publish." Your team will receive:
An email notification (you can include the Shared Document links from your Project app here).
An ICS file that allows them to sync their shifts directly to their personal phone calendars.

template.h3: Manage your agenda with the calendar app 📆
template.p: If you frequently manage activities and meetings in Odoo, you can synchronize them with your personal calendar to keep your schedule consistent across all devices.
To integrate tools like Google Calendar or Outlook, follow these simple steps:

li.p: Access Settings: Navigate to Settings > Calendar > Sync External Calendar.
Establish the Connection: Link each user’s external account to the Odoo Calendar app.

template.p: Once connected, any appointment made outside of Odoo will automatically reflect in your Odoo dashboard, and vice versa.
For a detailed walkthrough of the configuration, please refer to the following resources:
Ensure every billable item is invoiced so that nothing is missed.

ul.li: Confirm the quotation or rentals to generate an invoice automatically.
Send the invoice directly to the client from Odoo.

div.class: o_editor_banner user-select-none o-contenteditable-false lh-1 d-flex align-items-center alert alert-info pb-0 pt-3
o_editor_banner_content o-contenteditable-true w-100 px-3
div.p: Using down payments for Event Booking:

li.p: When generating an invoice from the quotation, you can request a down payment, either as a fixed amount or a percentage of the total (e.g., €1000 upfront to secure the venue and catering services).

Final Payment: After the event is completed, invoice the client for the remaining balance, which can include additional services or time tracked (e.g., extra hours, added logistics, or last-minute requests).

div.class: o_editor_banner user-select-none lh-1 d-flex align-items-center alert alert-success pb-0 pt-3 o-contenteditable-false
w-100 px-3 o_editor_banner_content o-contenteditable-true
div.p: You completed that demo use case! There are millions of other ways to adapt your Odoo setup to fit your business needs.
Discover more about Odoo by diving into our documentation and academy, and feel free to request a demo if you need help!

template.p: Beyond client projects, you can use the Events app to organize internal company gatherings, team-building sessions, or workshops. This app provides a full suite of tools to manage your event from announcement to check-in:

li.p: Online Presence: Create beautiful event pages and publish them directly to your website.
Flexible Ticketing: Configure multiple ticket tiers (e.g., Early Bird, VIP, or General Admission) with custom pricing.
Automated Booking: Enable online registration to let attendees book their spots instantly.
Attendee Management: Track your guest list in real-time and send digital tickets/badges via email automatically.
Rapid Entry: Use Odoo’s built-in scanning feature to manage high-traffic entrances and check in attendees in seconds.

template.p: and much more! Whether it's an intimate team lunch or a large-scale corporate conference, the Events app keeps everything organized and professional.

span.strong: Do you want to go further?

ul.li: Managing Your Own Venue & Location Bookings: If you own the venue where your events take place, you can use Odoo to manage its availability and even allow external clients to book the space online when you aren't using it.

div.class: o_editor_banner user-select-none o-contenteditable-false lh-1 d-flex align-items-center alert alert-info pb-0 pt-3
o_editor_banner_content o-contenteditable-true w-100 px-3
div.p: To ensure your space is never double-booked, follow these steps to link your venue to your online store:

li.p: Install eCommerce: Ensure the eCommerce app is active.
In the Rental app, create a new product (Here "My Location"):
Set the Product Type to Service.
In the Sales tab, set the Rental Periodicity to "Daily" (or hourly, depending on your needs).
In the Planning app, navigate to the Configuration tab to create a new Role, then click View to manage its settings. Ensure the following are configured correctly:
Resources: Set the number of resources that can be booked simultaneously (e.g., if you have two conference rooms, create two resources).
Services: Link the specific service product you want to track as "rented" within the Rental app.
Sync: Enable Sync Shifts and Rental Orders to activate the integration between the two modules.

div.p: Once this is set up and published on your website:

li.p: Live Availability: Customers can only book the dates where the venue is free.

Automatic Conflict Prevention: If you have already scheduled an internal event or a staff "Montage" (setup) in the Planning app for that location, Odoo will automatically gray out those dates on your website.

div.p: Now, your venue’s calendar manages itself!

ul.li: Want to handle ticket sales at the entrance? Install the Point of Sale app, add your tickets to it, and you're ready to go"
Enhance on-site communication with vendors by making calls and sending SMS or WhatsApp messages directly through Odoo, using our integrated VoIP and WhatsApp modules.

li.p: Use the Social Media app to manage and schedule social media posts related to your events if it's requested by the clients.

ul.li:
Manage your accounting more easily than ever with a completely integrated environment thanks to our
Accounting App.

div.class: o_editor_banner lh-1 d-flex align-items-center alert alert-info pb-0 pt-3 o-contenteditable-false
w-100 ms-3 o_editor_banner_content o-contenteditable-true
div.p: Would you like to discuss your Odoo setup with us or go even further?
